description: Authoritative advance widths (design units, 1000 UPM) for the Hildegard Neumes OTF. Keyed by font-internal glyph name. The generate-rhena-glyphs.py script asserts the OTF matches these to within `tolerance` units; mismatch is a build error. 2026-04-14 width review corrected three overflow bugs from the abandoned rhineland.rs (docs/planning/width-review-2026-04-14.md). 2026-04-19 SMuFL alignment (docs/adr/ADR-0009-smufl-alignment.md) adopted Bravura widths for tradition-agnostic geometric glyphs. 2026-04-19 trace-at-scale workflow adopted: calligraphic widths set from direct traces of manuscript reference images scaled to match the font's staff-space guidelines (1 ss = 250 du). Measured widths: rh_punctum 160 (body 130), rh_virga 120 (body 91), rh_pressus 300→240 (body 207×219), rh_punctum_inclinatum 120→140 (body 110×65), rh_quilisma 170→280 (body 247×276, significantly wider and taller than the earlier flat-ribbon spec), rh_strophicus 160→110 (body 73×54; daintier than inclinatum), rh_liquescent_desc (cephalicus) 160→150 (body 120×238; round-loop head + descending curl, TWO contours with interior void). 2026-04-19 rh_c_clef Bravura placeholder REPLACED with Rhineland trace-at-scale from c_clef.png: advance 134→150, body 122×158 (much more compact than Bravura's 134×442); font no longer depends on Bravura for any glyph outline. 2026-04-20 atom inventory expanded from 19→22: rh_pes_short (90 traced, integrated variant for pes-of-2nd), rh_pes_foot (90 traced), rh_pes_head (90 traced) added per ADR-0008 paleographic fidelity. Hildegard pes bottom is a hook (not a punctum) and top is a head without virga-terminator hook. The 125-du interval of a 2nd is smaller than any usable stub pair in the compositional path (stub_foot + stub_head = 151 du), so the Greciliae-style integrated short variant is required for 2nds. Rhena resolver: interval == 2nd → stamp PES_SHORT; interval ≥ 3rd → compose PES_FOOT + stretched PES_LINE + PES_HEAD. Widths finalized from user traces; rh_pes_line advance kept at 12 (compositional atom, advance is placeholder-irrelevant). See rhineland.contract.json compose_anchors for pitch/joint metadata. 2026-04-20 atom inventory expanded 22→25 with flexa compositional family paralleling the pes: rh_flexa_short (integrated variant for 2nd descending), rh_flexa_head, rh_flexa_foot (composition partners for 3rd-or-wider descending), and rh_flexa_line redesigned from a 172-du diagonal parallelogram to a vertical stem after paleographic review of image 17. Flexa atom widths finalized 90/90/90/12 from user trace-at-scale from images 16 (short) and 17 (long); rh_flexa_line advance kept at 12 (compositional-only placeholder). Flexa family uses a slightly narrower stem channel (x=[31, 69], 38 du) than pes (x=[36, 76], 40 du) per the per-reference traced scale. 2026-04-20 post-ADR-0012 revision: all Rhineland non-SMuFL atoms consolidated into PUA block U+F400..F40F (PRESSUS at U+F403, pes family U+F400..F402 + U+F404, flexa family U+F405..F408); pitch anchors normalized to pitch.x = stem.x within each compositional family so stems align automatically in composition; new stem_channel field on compositional atoms per rhineland.contract.json. widths.json carries advance-only data; compose_anchors and stem_channel are source-of-truth in the Rhena contract. Remaining calligraphic atoms (epiphonus, oriscus, deminutum, f_clef) pending references.
